en.summary: Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (AS) admonishes the believers to remain steadfast in their commitment to truth. The term 'al-mutalawwin' (the fickle one) refers to someone who constantly shifts allegiances and convictions according to personal advantage. This teaching, delivered to the Imam's close companions, warns that those who rule through falsehood will ultimately face ruin in both this world and the next.

key_terms.المتلون: al-Mutalawwin - The fickle one, a person who constantly changes color/position, unreliable in their commitments
key_terms.الحق: al-Haqq - The truth, what is right and just
key_terms.الباطل: al-Batil - Falsehood, that which is vain and without substance
key_terms.استبد: Istabadda - To seize absolute power, to rule tyrannically, to act despotically
key_terms.صاغرا: Saghiran - Humbled, debased, in a state of degradation

en.seo_question: What does Imam al-Sadiq say about fickleness and holding to the truth?

ur.summary: امام جعفر صادق علیہ السلام نے لوگوں کو نصیحت فرمائی کہ حق اور اہل حق سے ہرگز نہ ہٹیں۔ متلون (ڈانواں ڈول) شخص اللہ کے نزدیک مبغوض ہے۔ جو باطل کے ذریعے حکومت کرے وہ دنیا و آخرت دونوں میں ذلیل و رسوا ہوگا۔

key_terms.المتلون: متلون - ڈانواں ڈول شخص، رنگ بدلنے والا، ناقابل اعتماد
key_terms.استبد: استبداد کرنا - اکیلے حکم چلانا، ظلم سے حکومت کرنا

ur.seo_question: امام صادق علیہ السلام نے حق اور باطل کے بارے میں کیا فرمایا؟

tr.summary: İmam Cafer Sadık (a.s.) müminlere kararsızlıktan sakınmalarını ve hakka bağlı kalmalarını öğütlemiştir. 'Mütelevvin' (kararsız) kişi Allah katında buğzedilen biridir. Batıl yoluyla hükmetmeye kalkışan kimse hem helak olacak hem de dünyadan zelil olarak ayrılacaktır.
tr.seo_question: İmam Sadık kararsızlık ve hakka bağlılık hakkında ne buyurmuştur?

fa.summary: امام جعفر صادق علیه السلام به مؤمنان هشدار داده‌اند که از تلوّن (دمدمی‌مزاجی) بپرهیزند و از حق و اهل آن جدا نشوند. کسی که با باطل حکومت کند، هم در دنیا هلاک می‌شود و هم ذلیل و خوار از آن خارج خواهد شد.
fa.seo_question: امام صادق علیه السلام درباره ثبات قدم در حق چه فرمودند؟

id.summary: Imam Ja'far al-Shadiq (a.s.) menasihati kaum mukmin agar tetap berpegang pada kebenaran dan tidak menjadi orang yang berubah-ubah pendirian. Orang yang berubah-ubah dibenci Allah, dan siapa pun yang memerintah dengan kebatilan akan binasa dan meninggalkan dunia dalam kehinaan.

key_terms.المتلون: al-Mutalawwin - Orang yang berubah-ubah, tidak konsisten

id.seo_question: Apa yang dikatakan Imam al-Shadiq tentang orang yang berubah-ubah dan kebenaran?

bn.summary: ইমাম জাফর আস-সাদিক (আ.) মুমিনদের উপদেশ দিয়েছেন যে তারা যেন চঞ্চলতা পরিহার করে এবং সত্যে অবিচল থাকে।
bn.seo_question: ইমাম সাদিক চঞ্চলতা ও সত্যে অবিচল থাকা সম্পর্কে কী বলেছেন?

es.summary: El Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (P) exhorta a los creyentes a mantenerse firmes en la verdad. Allah detesta a la persona inconstante que cambia de posición según su conveniencia.
es.seo_question: ¿Qué dijo el Imam al-Sadiq sobre la inconstancia y la verdad?

fr.summary: L'Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (P) met en garde les croyants contre l'inconstance et les exhorte à rester fermement attachés à la vérité.
fr.seo_question: Que dit l'Imam al-Sadiq sur l'inconstance et la vérité ?

de.summary: Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (F) ermahnt die Gläubigen, standhaft an der Wahrheit festzuhalten. Der Begriff 'al-Mutalawwin' bezeichnet den Wankelmütigen.
de.seo_question: Was sagte Imam al-Sadiq über Wankelmut und Wahrheit?

ru.summary: Имам Джафар ас-Садик (мир ему) наставляет верующих быть стойкими в приверженности истине.
ru.seo_question: Что сказал Имам ас-Садик о непостоянстве и приверженности истине?

zh.summary: 伊玛目贾法尔·萨迪格（愿主赐他平安）告诫信士们要坚定不移地坚持真理。“穆塔拉维温”（变化无常之人）指的是不断改变立场的人，安拉憎恶此种人。凡借助虚妄进行统治者，必将灭亡并卑贱地离开今世。

key_terms.المتلون: 穆塔拉维温——变化无常之人

zh.seo_question: 伊玛目萨迪格关于反复无常和坚持真理说了什么？

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي أَبُو الْحَسَنِ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ الْكَاتِبُ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو عَلِيٍّ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ هَمَّامٍ الْإِسْكَافِيُّ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ الْحِمْيَرِيُّ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي أَحْمَدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَرْقِيُّ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي الْقَاسِمُ بْنُ يَحْيَى، عَنْ جَدَِّهِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ رَاشِدٍ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ، عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ قَالَ:
isnad_matn.matn_ar: اعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللَّهَ تَعَالَى يُبْغِضُ مِنْ خَلْقِهِ الْمُتَلَوَّنَ، فَلَا تَزُولُوا عَنِ الْحَقَّ وَأَهْلِهِ، فَإِنَّ مَنِ اسْتَبَدَّ بِالْبَاطِلِ وَأَهْلِهِ هَلَكَ، وَفَاتَتْهُ الدُّنْيَا وَخَرَجَ مِنْهَا صَاغِرًا.

narrators.name_ar: أَبُو الْحَسَنِ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ الْكَاتِبُ
narrators.name_en: Abul Hasan Ali ibn Ahmad ibn Ibrahim al-Katib
narrators.ambiguity_note: A narrator known in the Shia hadith tradition; his nisbah 'al-Katib' (the scribe) indicates a profession.
narrators.known_identity: Ali ibn Ahmad ibn Ibrahim al-Katib, a transmitter in the circle of al-Shaykh al-Mufid

narrators.name_ar: أَبُو عَلِيٍّ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ هَمَّامٍ الْإِسْكَافِيُّ
narrators.name_en: Abu Ali Muhammad ibn Hammam al-Iskafi
narrators.known_identity: Muhammad ibn Hammam ibn Suhayl al-Iskafi (d. 336 AH), a prominent Shia hadith scholar and author
Abdullah ibn Ja'far al-Himyari (d. after 290 AH), a major Shia hadith compiler from Qum

narrators.name_ar: أَحْمَدُ بْنُ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَرْقِيُّ
narrators.known_identity: Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid al-Barqi (d. 274 AH or 280 AH), author of al-Mahasin

narrators.ambiguity_note: Identified as al-Qasim ibn Yahya ibn al-Hasan ibn Rashid, who narrates from his grandfather.
narrators.known_identity: al-Qasim ibn Yahya ibn al-Hasan ibn Rashid, narrator from al-Barqi's circle

narrators.ambiguity_note: Referred to as the grandfather (jadd) of al-Qasim ibn Yahya.
narrators.known_identity: al-Hasan ibn Rashid, a Kufan narrator and grandfather of al-Qasim ibn Yahya
Muhammad ibn Muslim ibn Riyah al-Thaqafi al-Kufi (d. 150 AH), one of the most prominent companions of Imam al-Baqir and Imam al-Sadiq
Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (AS), the sixth Imam (d. 148 AH)

similar_content_hints.description: Hadiths from Imam Ali (AS) in Nahj al-Balagha about the importance of steadfastness in truth and the consequences of following falsehood.
Traditions about the qualities Allah despises in His servants, such as hypocrisy and inconsistency in faith.
Hadiths warning against tyranny and oppressive rule, and the inevitable downfall of those who rule through injustice.
